results.editorial:
Sunway Home Healthcare is a hospital-backed home care service operating under the Sunway Healthcare Group, one of Malaysia's larger private hospital networks. It is based at Sunway GEO Avenue in Bandar Sunway, Subang Jaya, and delivers care directly to patients' homes rather than admitting them to a residential facility. The service is positioned as an extension of Sunway Medical Centre's clinical standards into the community setting.

The team includes registered nurses with post-basic training in specialist areas. Services cover wound care, tube feeding (NGT), stoma care, oncology patient management, post-stroke and post-surgery rehabilitation, home physiotherapy, and palliative end-of-life nursing. Palliative patients are typically referred in by a Sunway Medical Centre physician. Pricing for individual sessions is listed on the website and ranges from around RM 160 to RM 380 or more depending on the service, though package costs vary. Day care and hourly respite sessions are also available.

This is not a residential nursing home — there are no beds, rooms, or 24-hour on-site staff. It is the right fit if your family member is living at home or with relatives and you need skilled clinical support on a scheduled basis: wound rounds, tube changes, post-hospitalisation monitoring, or caregiver respite. For families needing round-the-clock supervision or a residential placement, this service alone will not meet that need. Operating hours are Monday to Friday, 8:30am to 5:30pm; after-hours availability should be confirmed directly.

facts.care_types: Home nursing, palliative care, home physiotherapy, oncology care, post-stroke care, respite care
facts.red_flags: Not a residential facility — no on-site overnight care. After-hours coverage unconfirmed.

results.editorial:
Columbia Asia Extended Care Hospital in Section 17, Shah Alam, is the oldest facility in the Columbia Asia private hospital group — opened in 1998 and reportedly the group's first Malaysian property. Unlike the group's other acute hospitals, this facility is designed from the ground up for extended stays: long-term nursing care, post-hospitalisation step-down, and inpatient rehabilitation, rather than acute surgery or emergency medicine. It is licensed and operates as a private hospital with resident and on-call doctors.

The hospital has 65 beds and provides 24-hour nursing care alongside physiotherapy, occupational therapy, and speech therapy. Published rehabilitation packages give an indication of pricing: a 10-day Neuro Recovery Package is listed at RM 7,500 and a 30-day version at RM 30,000; an Orthopaedic Recovery Package starts at RM 3,000. Long-term nursing placements for conditions such as cancer or post-stroke dependence are also offered. Doctor house-call services extend beyond the hospital walls. The facility has visiting hours (11:30am–2:30pm and 4:30pm–8:30pm), which suggests a reasonably structured clinical environment.

This is a strong option for families who need a medically supervised step-down placement after an acute hospital stay — particularly for neurological recovery or complex nursing cases where a standard residential home may lack clinical depth. The pricing tier is higher than most community nursing homes; clarify whether your family member's needs fit the rehabilitation pathway or long-term nursing track, as the cost structures differ. Insurance coverage through major providers is worth confirming before admission.

facts.care_types: Long-term nursing care, inpatient rehabilitation, palliative care, post-hospitalisation step-down, respite care
facts.red_flags: Higher price point than community nursing homes. Confirm insurance coverage and which care pathway applies before admission.

results.editorial:
Amazing Grace Senior Home is a residential elder care facility located at Jalan SS19/4 in SS19, Subang Jaya. It operates under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand, which also runs a separate property in Section 14, Petaling Jaya. The SS19 home is listed as a retirement home on Google Maps, and published third-party listings place it in the category of assisted living rather than high-dependency nursing care.

Third-party pricing aggregators list room options ranging from RM 2,001 to RM 4,000 per month across Amazing Grace's locations, with the SS19 branch at the higher end of that range (RM 3,001–RM 4,000). Room types available include single rooms with and without attached toilet, twin rooms, and triple occupancy. Reviewers note clean surroundings, responsive staff, and regular monitoring of vital signs. Daily exercise is offered, including for residents with mobility limitations. The home reports not using a computer-based resident management system, which is worth factoring in if you need structured documentation.

This home suits relatively mobile or semi-mobile seniors needing supported daily living in a residential setting, rather than complex medical or dementia care. Families should verify directly whether nursing staff are on-site overnight, what the policy is for residents who deteriorate medically, and whether JKM registration is in place. Given it operates across two sites, also confirm which management team covers the SS19 branch.

facts.red_flags: No computer-based resident management system reported. Verify overnight nursing cover, JKM registration, and which branch team is on-site.

results.editorial:
My Aged Care's My Manor is the flagship property of My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, a licensed elder care operator in Petaling Jaya described on its own website as established in 2008. My Manor sits behind the MBPJ City Council building in PJS 7, occupies roughly 10,000 square feet, and is built around a low-density concept — fewer residents per floor than typical multi-storey nursing homes. The company now runs five Petaling Jaya locations in total, with My Manor being the original.

Care tiers at My Manor span general assisted living, bedridden care, post-stroke rehabilitation, post-operative recovery, and palliative care. The website states 24-hour care with hourly rounds and a doctor on-call arrangement. Six meals daily and laundry are included. The facility also offers home nursing and ambulance services as an extension of its main care offering. An in-house physiotherapy programme supports residents recovering from stroke or surgery.

Pricing was not published on the facility's own site, but third-party sources list room options across the My Aged Care network ranging from RM 3,001 to RM 5,000 per month depending on room type and care level. Families considering My Manor should confirm current bed availability directly — the low-density model means capacity is limited. Ask specifically about the doctor on-call arrangement (response times, whether a GP or specialist), nurse-to-resident ratios at night, and JKM registration status.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, assisted living, post-stroke rehab, palliative care, home nursing
facts.red_flags: Pricing not published. Confirm overnight nurse cover, doctor on-call response times, and JKM status.

results.editorial:
Amazing Grace Retirement Home is located at 11, Jalan 14/55 in Section 14, Petaling Jaya, and is one of two properties operated under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand in the Petaling Jaya–Subang Jaya corridor. The Section 14 address is a residential area with strong transport links, which makes hospital follow-ups and family visits manageable. The facility is listed under nursing home, assisted living, and physiotherapy on Google Maps, suggesting a higher care ceiling than the SS19 sister property.

The website and third-party sources describe wound care, bedsore management, pain management, physiotherapy, and 24-hour observation as offered services. Short-term, long-term, day care, and intermittent care options are available. The physical environment is described as including garden space and a massage chair. Published pricing from third-party aggregators places the facility in the RM 2,001–RM 4,000 per month range. Reviewers highlight consistent vital signs monitoring and daily exercise assistance for residents with mobility limitations.

The facility appears appropriate for seniors needing nursing-level support — wound care, bedsore prevention, post-hospitalisation monitoring — in a quieter residential setting. The Google Maps listing includes 'Physical therapist' as a care type, so verify whether physiotherapy is provided by an in-house registered physiotherapist or an external contractor visiting on a schedule. Also confirm JKM registration, whether nursing staff are on-site overnight, and the process for escalating residents who deteriorate to acute care.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, assisted living, physiotherapy, day care
facts.red_flags: Confirm whether physiotherapy is in-house or contracted. Verify JKM registration and overnight nursing cover.

results.editorial:
Synergy Care Centre is a 24-hour nursing home at 15, Jalan USJ Sentral 3, in the USJ Sentral commercial precinct of Subang Jaya, Selangor. The facility is operated under Nursing Synergy Sdn Bhd, which also maintains a separate contact line at a second location. The centre is led by Dr. Pathma, who is referenced in multiple reviews as being personally involved in resident care and responsive to family enquiries.

The centre provides trained registered nurse coverage around the clock, with a senior physiotherapist on-site delivering daily physiotherapy seven days a week. Clinical capabilities include feeding tube management, tracheostomy care and suctioning, oxygen therapy, wound care, and medication administration. Room types include single rooms, twin rooms, and dormitory beds, with third-party pricing sources listing rates in the RM 2,001–RM 3,000 per month range. Day care for seniors who return home each evening is also offered. Social activities — described as yoga, gardening, drawing, and board games — are organised regularly.

This is a competent option for medically complex residents within the USJ area, particularly those requiring tube care or respiratory support who cannot afford acute hospital costs long-term. The seven-days-a-week physiotherapy is a meaningful differentiator at this price band. Confirm on-site nurse staffing ratios, whether the doctor visit is a dedicated medical director or an on-call arrangement, and current JKM registration status before committing.

facts.red_flags: Pricing at lower end — confirm staffing ratios and whether doctor is on-call or on-site. Verify JKM status.

results.editorial:
My Precious Home Care Elder Care is located at 27, Jalan USJ 1/3B, in the Grandville residential area of Subang Jaya. The facility was founded by Madam Ng, whose motivation came from personal experience caring for her own ageing parents. The website states the centre was 'Founded by Medical Professionals,' with a doctor conducting regular resident assessments — though the specific clinical structure around that oversight is not detailed publicly.

Services include 24-hour personal care, dementia care, palliative care, short-term respite, rehabilitation, and physiotherapy. Four home-cooked meals are served daily. All rooms have attached bathrooms, and wheelchair access is noted. The facility positions itself as a single low-density residential property — a deliberate contrast to larger institutional nursing homes — which typically means fewer total beds and more hands-on supervision per resident.

Pricing is not published on the website. This is a reasonable choice for families who want a smaller, home-like environment with medical professional oversight rather than a larger institutional setting. Before visiting, clarify the number of residents currently in care, the number of care staff on shift overnight, what dementia-specific accommodations are in place, and whether the physiotherapy is provided by a registered physiotherapist or a trained care aide. JKM registration should also be confirmed.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, respite care, day care
facts.red_flags: Pricing unpublished. Confirm resident count, overnight staffing, JKM status, and physiotherapy qualifications.

results.editorial:
Mintygreen Nursing Home's Sungai Long branch — branded as the Kajang ElderCare Center and operating as the group's original headquarters — is located at Landmark Residences 2, Jalan Sungai Long, Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang. The Mintygreen Healthcare Group was founded in 2017, starting in a bungalow in Desa Permai before expanding to a six-unit shoplot in Sungai Long and subsequently growing to seven branches across KL, Selangor, and Penang. The group describes itself as the first senior care chain in Malaysia to integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) as a core service.

Care ranges from semi-dependent to fully bedridden residents, with short-term and long-term placement options and day care available. Clinical services include bi-weekly visiting GP consultations, NG tube feeding and changing, stoma and catheter care, insulin administration, wound and pressure ulcer management, and oxygen therapy. TCM services — acupuncture and Tui Na — are offered as add-ons. The Sungai Long branch is within 10–15 minutes of several hospitals including Columbia Asia Cheras, UKM Medical Centre, and KPJ Kajang. Long-term stay pricing starts from RM 4,000 per month; day care is listed at RM 220 per day.

The TCM integration is uncommon in the Malaysian nursing home sector and may suit families who prefer a complementary approach alongside conventional nursing. The Sungai Long branch draws from the Kajang and Cheras catchment areas. Ask about current bed availability (the shoplot format limits total capacity), JKM registration, and whether the visiting doctor schedule can be adjusted for residents with higher medical needs.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, day care, rehabilitation, physiotherapy, TCM (acupuncture, Tui Na), palliative care, hospice
facts.red_flags: Limited bed count in shoplot format. Confirm JKM registration, current capacity, and frequency of doctor visits.

results.editorial:
PJ Senior Care is a nursing home at 23, Jalan 14/56 in Seksyen 14, Petaling Jaya — a short distance from the Amazing Grace Retirement Home on Jalan 14/55, making Section 14 a notable cluster of elder care options in PJ. The facility's stated focus is on geriatric, orthopaedic, and neurological rehabilitation, with a team the website describes as comprising physiotherapists, nurses, and doctors trained specifically in stroke and mobility care.

The mission statement emphasises using physiotherapy and nursing together to improve both physical and mental wellbeing. In-house physiotherapy, geriatric nursing, medication management, nutritional guidance, and hospital follow-ups are listed as core services. The facility also organises festival celebrations and birthday events for residents. A standby doctor is noted as available on-site. The operating schedule is Monday to Sunday, 10am to 7pm, which raises the question of how overnight medical coverage is structured — this should be confirmed.

Pricing and bed count are not published. The rehabilitation emphasis makes PJ Senior Care worth considering for post-stroke or post-orthopaedic surgery recovery cases within the PJ area, particularly families who want physiotherapy included rather than contracted separately. On a site visit, ask about the doctor's visiting frequency and after-hours protocol, overnight nurse staffing, and JKM registration. Confirm whether the physiotherapy programme is designed around individual patient goals or is a group exercise format.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, physiotherapy, geriatric rehab, stroke rehabilitation
facts.red_flags: Operating hours listed as 10am–7pm daily — clarify overnight medical and nursing coverage. JKM status unconfirmed.

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Al-Fikrah Malaysia is a welfare home — not a fee-based nursing home — located at Lot 683, Jalan Sekolah, Kampung Sungai Sekamat, off Jalan Cheras, Kajang, Selangor. It was established in August 2013 and is run as an NGO under a committee chaired by Haji Mohd Azmi Bin Mohd Zain. The home is Islamic in character and serves a mix of populations: elderly residents without family support, chronically ill individuals, converts to Islam, people with disabilities, and those discharged from hospital with nowhere to go. The current capacity is approximately 70 residents.

The centre accepts both men and women. Basic medical services are provided through outreach — UKM Medical Centre's community health unit and other university faculties have conducted health screening visits, suggesting some external medical support, though the home does not appear to operate with resident nurses or doctors on permanent staff. Residents participate in vocational activities including gardening and small-scale animal husbandry. Funding comes from government grants, organisational donations, and public contributions via Islamic bank accounts (Maybank Islamic and Bank Muamalat).

This home serves a different function from a private nursing home. It is appropriate for elderly Muslims who have no family support network and no means to afford private care — or for families who need a placement of last resort. It is not designed for high-dependency nursing, dementia specialisation, or complex clinical care. Families seeking a placement here should contact the committee directly to understand current capacity, admission criteria, and what level of ongoing family involvement is expected or possible.

facts.care_types: Welfare home (elderly, disabled, chronically ill, converts)
facts.red_flags: Not a conventional fee-based nursing home. No permanent nursing staff confirmed. Primarily donation-funded — verify current capacity and admission criteria.
